title: Bit++
description: You are given n statements in the Bit++ programming language. There is a variable x which starts at 0. Each statement is one of: "++X", "X++", "--X", or "X--". The first two increment x by 1, and the last two decrement x by 1. Determine the final value of x after all statements are executed.
input_spec: The first line contains an integer n (1 <= n <= 150) -- the number of statements. Each of the next n lines contains a single statement ("++X", "X++", "--X", or "X--").
output_spec: Print the final value of x.

routes.name: Check for + or -
routes.description: For each statement, determine if it's an increment or decrement by looking for the '+' or '-' character.

observations.title: Only two operations matter
observations.description: Despite four different statement forms, there are only two effects: increment (+1) or decrement (-1). The prefix vs postfix distinction doesn't matter here.

hints.nudge: Do you need to distinguish between prefix and postfix forms?
hints.question: What is the actual difference between ++X and X++ in this problem? Does it affect the result?
hints.formal: Both ++X and X++ add 1 to x. Both --X and X-- subtract 1 from x. Only the sign matters, not the position.

observations.title: Detect the sign character
observations.description: Each statement is exactly 3 characters. You can check if the statement contains '+' to determine increment, or '-' for decrement.

hints.nudge: What character distinguishes increment statements from decrement statements?
hints.question: If you look at any position in the 3-character string, can you find '+' or '-'? Which position is easiest?
hints.formal: Check if '+' is in the statement (or check statement[1] which is always the operator). If '+', add 1; if '-', subtract 1.

observations.title: Accumulate and output
observations.description: Start x at 0, apply each operation, and print the final value.

hints.nudge: What is the initial value of x?
hints.question: How do you keep a running total as you process each statement?
hints.formal: x = 0. For each statement: if '+' in statement: x += 1 else: x -= 1. Print x.
